faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in Gramercy Park buildings?
faqs.answer: Gramercy Park's historic pre-war buildings and brownstones often have older plumbing, higher humidity levels, and numerous cracks and crevices where silverfish thrive. These structures also tend to have basements, storage areas, and libraries that provide ideal dark, damp environments for silverfish to breed and feed on paper, fabrics, and starches.

faqs.question: How quickly can you eliminate a silverfish infestation in my Gramercy Park apartment?
faqs.answer: Most silverfish infestations in Gramercy Park can be significantly reduced within 2-3 weeks, with complete elimination typically achieved within 4-6 weeks. Treatment time depends on the severity of the infestation and moisture levels in your space. We provide follow-up visits to ensure all silverfish and their eggs are eradicated.
